code: string_db_filepath <- "your/filepath/here.tsv"  # optional check that the nodenames are consistent with STRINGdb sym.map <- StandardizeGeneSymbols(gene.cccn.nodes) identical (unique(sym.map$standard_symbol), gene.cccn.nodes)  # TRUE so no further action is required. # If there were differences, replace symbol.map = NULL with symbol.map = sym.map   stringdb.edges <- GetSTRINGdb.edges(        gene.cccn.edges,                                             gene.cccn.nodes,                                             local = TRUE,                                             string.local.path = string_db_filepath,                                             combined.score.threshold = 400,                                             include.transferred = TRUE,                                             symbol.map = NULL) stringdb.edges <- BRCA_stringdb.edges head(stringdb.edges) >>   source target             interaction Weight >> 1   AAK1  AGFG1                database    500 >> 2   AAK1    GAK             experiments    411 >> 3   AAK1  ITSN1                database    500 >> 4   AAK1  NUMBL experiments_transferred    322 >> 5   AAK1  STK11 experiments_transferred     46 >> 6   ABL1   ABL2             experiments   1431 genemania_db_filepath <- "your/filepath/here.tsv"  genemania.edges <- GetGeneMANIA.edges (gm.all.edges.path,                                 gene.cccn.nodes,                                 local                = TRUE,                                 genemania.local.path = genemania_db_filepath,                                 gm.interaction.types = c("Pathway", "Physical Interactions", "Predicted")) genemania.edges <- BRCA_genemania.edges head(genemania.edges) >>          source  target interaction Weight >> 4815149  CAMK2B  CAMK2D     Pathway   0.50 >> 4815150  CAMK2B  MAP3K7     Pathway   0.50 >> 4815191 CSNK2A1  CSNK2B     Pathway   0.42 >> 4815192 CSNK2A2 CSNK2A1     Pathway   0.55 >> 4815193 CSNK2A2  CSNK2B     Pathway   0.42 >> 4815223    EGFR  MAP2K1     Pathway   0.13 file_path <- system.file("extdata", "Kinase_Substrate_Dataset.txt", package = "PTMsToPathways") kinsub.edges <- GetKinsub.edges(file_path, gene.cccn.nodes)  # Now we can build the CFN. network.list <- BuildClusterFilteredNetwork(gene.cccn.edges,                                             stringdb.edges,                                             genemania.edges,                                             kinsub.edges,                                             db.filepaths = c())  combined.PPIs <- network.list[[1]] cfn <- network.list[[2]] dim(cfn)  cfn.merged <- mergeEdges(cfn) dim(cfn.merged) >> [1] 9965    4 >> [1] 2810    4 bioplanet.file <- system.file("extdata", "bioplanet_pathway_June2025.csv", package = "PTMsToPathways") PCN.data <- BuildPathwayCrosstalkNetwork(common.clusters, bioplanet.file,                                          createfile = FALSE) PCN.data <- BRCA_PCN.data pathway.crosstalk.network <- PCN.data[[1]] # what: Evaluate Cluster Quality from PTM Data — EvaluateClusters
title: Evaluate Cluster Quality from PTM Data — EvaluateClusters
text: Computes set diagnostic statistics composite quality index cluster cluster list, using original PTM data matrix reference.  function consolidates four previous evaluation functions (clust.eval.1, nbclust.eval, lincsclust.eval, clust.eval) single interface controlled three arguments: data.type, index.mode, use.slope.

title: Evaluate Cluster Quality from PTM Data — EvaluateClusters
code: EvaluateClusters(   clusterlist,   tbl.sc,   data.type = c("ratio", "intensity", "count"),   index.mode = c("density", "size"),   use.slope = TRUE,   ratio.col.pattern = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Evaluate Cluster Quality from PTM Data — EvaluateClusters
text: clusterlist named unnamed list clusters returned MakeClusterList().  element must character vector gene / PTM names data frame column named PTMnames (format produced MakeClusterList). tbl.sc Numeric data matrix PTMs / genes row names samples column names.  table passed MakeClusterList(). data.type Character string specifying nature input data. One : "ratio" (default) TMT log2 ratios; values may negative. Absolute values used internally diagnostic calculations signal magnitude assessed without sign cancellation. "intensity" Non-negative raw TMT intensities peptide abundance values.  absolute-value transformation applied. "count" Non-negative peptide count data (original clust.eval.1 behaviour).  Treated identically "intensity". index.mode Character string selecting index formula.  One "density" (default, recommended) "size".  See Index formula section . use.slope Logical.  TRUE (default), apply slope-based outlier filter (see Slope filter section).  Set FALSE unordered cohort data BRCA TMT proteomics, sample order arbitrary. ratio.col.pattern Character string NULL (default).  regular expression passed base::grep() identify remove ratio columns computing diagnostic statistics.  example, LINCS data may contain columns named "H1_to_control" whose name encodes ratio relationship; passing "" removes columns evaluation. Set NULL (default) skip step. verbose Logical.  TRUE (default), print progress messages cluster.  Set FALSE suppress output batch workflows.

title: Evaluate Cluster Quality from PTM Data — EvaluateClusters
text: data frame sorted descending order Index, ascending percent.NA.  Columns : Group Integer cluster index (1-based, matching clusterlist position). .genes Number genes / PTMs cluster. culled..slope Number rows flagged slope filter (0 use.slope = FALSE). percent.singlesamplegenes Percentage genes / PTMs detected one sample. .samples Number samples contain least one non-missing value gene cluster. percent.singlegenesamples Percentage samples contain non-missing value one gene cluster. total.signal Sum absolute signal values across cluster sub-table (NA removal , applicable, absolute-value transformation). percent.NA Percentage missing values cluster sub-table restricted samples least one non-missing value. intensity Total signal scaling missing-value fraction (total.signal * (1 - percent.NA / 100)).  included index.mode = "density". Index Composite quality index (see Index formula section). Higher values indicate better clusters.

title: Evaluate Cluster Quality from PTM Data — EvaluateClusters
text: Two index modes available, selected index.mode: "size" (original nbclust.eval / clust.eval) Rewards large, information-dense clusters. Index = intensity * (1 + realsamples) * (1 + cleargenes) / (1 + percent.NA) "density" (original lincsclust.eval; recommended default) Rewards data density per gene.  Compact, information-rich clusters score higher large diffuse ones. Index = (1 + realsamples) * (1 + cleargenes) / ((1 + percent.NA) * .genes) : intensity = total signal removing NA fraction realsamples = samples single-gene samples cleargenes = genes culled slope filter percent.NA = percentage missing values cluster sub-table

title: Evaluate Cluster Quality from PTM Data — EvaluateClusters
text: use.slope = TRUE, cluster sub-table re-ordered columns (samples) sorted decreasing total signal rows (genes/PTMs) sorted decreasing total signal.  linear model fit row column rank.  Rows positive undefined slope flagged "bad slope" counted culled..slope.  rows contribute index positive slope (signal increasing decreasing sample rank) inconsistent pattern expected coherent cluster.  Set use.slope = FALSE unordered cohort data (e.g. BRCA TMT) sample order arbitrary slope filter meaningful.

title: Heatmap of Phosphopeptides Grouped by Cluster — graph.ptm.by.cluster
text: Creates large phosphopeptide heatmap colored signal intensity, grouped consensus cluster high-contrast cluster color bars. Supports custom row ordering optional legend/heatkey output files.

title: Heatmap of Phosphopeptides Grouped by Cluster — graph.ptm.by.cluster
code: graph.ptm.by.cluster(   ptmtable,   common.clusters,   filename = "ptm_by_cluster_heatmap.pdf",   legend.filename = NULL,   heatkey.filename = NULL,   cluster.order = names(common.clusters),   order.rows = c("as.is", "mean", "slope", "hclust"),   cluster.cols = FALSE,   zlim = 3,   min.shared = 2,   show.row.labels = FALSE,   show.col.labels = TRUE,   row_cex = 0.2,   col_cex = 0.8,   main = "Phosphopeptides grouped by cluster" )

title: Heatmap of Phosphopeptides Grouped by Cluster — graph.ptm.by.cluster
text: ptmtable numeric matrix PTMs rows samples columns. common.clusters named list element character vector PTM identifiers belonging cluster. filename Output filename main heatmap PDF. Defaults "ptm_by_cluster_heatmap.pdf". legend.filename Optional filename separate PDF cluster legend. NULL, legend file written. heatkey.filename Optional filename separate PDF color scale key. NULL, heatkey file written. cluster.order Character vector specifying order clusters displayed. Defaults names(common.clusters). order.rows Method order rows within clusters: "." (reordering), "mean" (row-wise mean), "slope" (linear trend), "hclust" (hierarchical clustering). Defaults ".". cluster.cols Logical; TRUE, columns also hierarchically clustered. Defaults FALSE. zlim Numeric; upper/lower limit heatmap color scale (values clipped [-zlim, zlim]). Defaults 3. min.shared Minimum number shared finite values required compute distance two samples. Defaults 2. show.row.labels Logical; show row labels (PTM identifiers). Defaults FALSE. show.col.labels Logical; show column labels (sample names). Defaults TRUE. row_cex Character expansion factor row labels. Defaults 0.2. col_cex Character expansion factor column labels. Defaults 0.8. main Plot title. Defaults "Phosphopeptides grouped cluster".

title: Heatmap of Phosphopeptides Grouped by Cluster — graph.ptm.by.cluster
text: Invisibly returns list containing: mat: clustered expression matrix. blocks: List ordered sub-matrices, one per cluster. rowsep: Row indices separating clusters. cluster.palette: Named vector cluster colors.

title: Extracts co-clustered PTM and gene network from a cluster filtered network object. — ptms_to_cfn
code: # Example input objects ptms <- c("TP53 p S15", "BRCA1 p S123; BRCA1 p T124", "MDM2 p S200") # Example network # edge list with 'source', 'target', and cluster info, could be data.frame or igraph cfn.merged <- data.frame(source = c("TP53", "BRCA1", "BRCA1", "MDM2"),                          target = c("BRCA1", "MDM2", "TP53", "TP53"),                          cluster = c(1,1,2,1)) # Suppose filter.edges.0 and get.co.clustered.ptms are also defined and loaded # The following returns the gene/PTM subnetwork res <- ptms_to_cfn(ptms, cfn = cfn.merged, pepsep = ";") #> Error in ptms_to_cfn(ptms, cfn = cfn.merged, pepsep = ";"): object 'cfn.merged' not found print(res) #> Error: object 'res' not found
